title: Ch 9.2 — Multiple Catch & Finally: The Evil ATM
description:
Practice using multiple catch blocks and a finally block in one method.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Background
──────────────────────────────────────────────

The Bank of Evil ATM processes withdrawal requests. A customer
types in a withdrawal amount as text. Two things can go wrong:

  • The text isn't a number (e.g., "twenty")  → NumberFormatException
  • The number is zero or negative             → IllegalArgumentException
    (you throw this yourself)

The ATM must also always print a log message, no matter what happens.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Your Task: complete processWithdrawal()
──────────────────────────────────────────────

  Method signature (already provided):
    public String processWithdrawal(String amountText)

  Declare  String result = "";  before your try block.

  TRY:
    1. Parse amountText to a double.
    2. If amount <= 0, throw:
         new IllegalArgumentException("Amount must be positive")
    3. If both pass, set:
         result = "Dispensing $" + amount

  CATCH NumberFormatException:
    result = "Error: not a valid number"

  CATCH IllegalArgumentException:
    result = "Error: " + e.getMessage()

  FINALLY:
    result += " | ATM logged."

  Return result.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Double.parseDouble("twenty") throws NumberFormatException.
• You create and throw IllegalArgumentException yourself
  with the  throw  keyword.
• The finally block runs whether an exception happened or not,
  so " | ATM logged." will always be appended to result.
• Return result AFTER the entire try-catch-finally block.
